title: Ramprasad vs Shyamvati on 30 March, 2022
body: Ramprasad vs Shyamvati on 30 March, 2022 Author: Vishal Mishra Bench: Vishal Mishra 1 IN THE HIGH COURT OF MADHYA PRADESH AT JABALPUR BEFORE HON'BLE SHRI JUSTICE VISHAL MISHRA ON THE 30th OF MARCH, 2022 MISC. PETITION No. 6653 of 2019 Between:- RAMPRASAD S/O LATE NOKHE BONIA , AGED ABOUT 90 YEARS, OCCUPATION: THR. NEXT FRIEND TULSIRAM BONIA S/O RAMPRASAD A/A 52 KUKDACHIMN TAHSIL MOHKHED CHHINDWARA, THR. R/O VILLAGE KUKDA CHIMAN TAHSIL AND DISTT. CHHINDWARA (MADHYA PRADESH) .....PETITIONER (BY SHRI JAIDEEP SIRPURKAR, ADVOCATE ) AND 1. SHYAMVATI D/O LATE NOKHE BONIA W/O GHOODO SIMANKAR , AGED ABOUT 87 YEARS, KUKDACHIMAN TAHSIL MOHKHED CHHINDWARA (MADHYA PRADESH) 2. JUNNI ALIAS TUSIA D/O LATE NOKHE BONIA W/O GUNNU SHAHARE , AGED ABOUT 83 YEARS, R/O SILLEWANI, TAHSIL MOHKHED (MADHYA PRADESH) 3. YASHODA D/O FAGGYA W/O ROSHANLAL GONDANE , AGED ABOUT 50 YEARS, R/O TEK MOHALLA DONGAR PARASIA, TAHSIL PARASIA (MADHYA PRADESH) 4. VIVEKANAND S/O BAISAKHU SHENDE , AGED ABOUT 45 YEARS, R/O RANGEENKHAPA, TEHSIL AND DISTT CHHINDWARA (MADHYA PRADESH) 5. SHARMILA D/O BAISAKHU SHENDE , AGED ABOUT 40 YEARS, R/O RANGEENKHAPA, TEHSIL AND DISTT CHHINDWARA (MADHYA PRADESH) 6. AJAY MAHORE S/O SHEETAL PRASAD MAHORE , AGED ABOUT 26 YEARS, R/O KUKDACHIMAN, TAHSIL MOHKHED (MADHYA PRADESH) 7. TEHSILDAR TAHSIL MOHKHED DISTT- CHHINDWARA (MADHYA PRADESH) 8. STATE OF MADHYA PRADESH THR. THE C O L L E C T O R DISTT-CHHINDWARA (MADHYA PRADESH) Signature Not Verified SAN .....RESPONDENTS (BY SHRI PIYUSH JAIN, GOVT. ADVOCATE FOR STATE ) Digitally signed by ANINDYA SUNDAR MUKHOPADHYAY Date: 2022.04.04 14:38:37 IST T h is petition coming on for admission this day, the court passed the 2 following: ORDER Despite service on respondents No.1 to 7 nobody has tendered their appearance before this Court. The petition is pending since 2019 and interim relief granted whereby the proceedings of the civil court has been stayed. In such circumstances, this Court deems it appropriate to take up the matter for hearing. Challenge being made to the order dated 04.11.2019 passed by the Second Additional Judge to the Court of First Civil Judge Class-II Chhindwara, whereby the application filed under Order 32 Rule 5 read with Section 147 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 for appointment of a next friend in the suit before the Trial Court has been rejected. It is argued that the petitioner has filed a civil suit seeking declaration of title and confirmation of possession, sale deed executed inter se between the parties as not binding upon the petitioner and for permanent injunction. On summons being issued, a joint written statements have been filed by the respondents Nos. 3, 4 and 5. During the pendency of the civil suit an application is filed by the petitioner under Order 32 Rule 5 read with Section 147 of Code of Civil Procedure for appointment of next friend of the petitioner on the ground that he is an old age person and physically and mentally weak, therefore, not in a proper position and also to some extent has lost his memory due to old age, therefore, he is unable to understand the consequences of the civil suit. The reply to the aforesaid application was filed on the ground that the petitioner is filing an application just to extend the proceedings of the civil suit and to escape from evidence. The Trial Court vide impugned order has rejected the application solely on the ground that there is no provision for granting of next friend to an old and infirm person. The provisions of Order 32 Rule 5 of C.P.C . are only applicable in the case of a minor, therefore, the application was rejected. The Supreme Court in the case of Kasturi Bai and others Vs. Anguri Chaudhary reported in (2003) AIR (SC) 1773, wherein the similar aspect was Signature Not Verified taken into consideration and the next friend was appointed considering the old age SAN of the party and looking to the mental condition. It is argued that the learned Trial Digitally signed by ANINDYA SUNDAR MUKHOPADHYAY Date: 2022.04.04 14:38:37 IST Court prior to rejection of an application should have enquired into the fact 3 regarding incapacity of a person to protect his/her own interest due to mental infirmity as a result of age. He has further placed reliance upon the judgment passed by this Court in the case of Kashi Bai and another Vs. Kashiram and others reported in (2001) 1 MPHT 239 wherein, the similar aspect was taken into consideration by this Court. He has prayed that similar relief be extended to him and the order impugned be set aside. Heard the learned counsel for the petitioner and perused the record. From the perusal of the record, it is not disputed that the civil suit is pending consideration wherein the defendants have already tendered their appearance by filing a written statement. The petitioner is aged about 90 years and during the pendency of the civil suit, he has filed an application under Order 32 Rule 5 read with Section 147 of C.P.C. for appointment of a next friend to enable him to brief the case on his behalf. On the perusal of the application, it is seen that the grounds for appointment of next friend are that he is an old and infirm person and owing to the age he is not in a position to speak properly and move and even it is difficult for him to perform his day to day activities and his mental condition is also weak as a result of age factor. The provisions of Order 32 Rule 5 of C.P.C . are applicable in case of a minor, but the provisions of Order 32 Rule 15 of C.P.C . could have been exercised by the learned Trial Court read with Section 147 of C.P.C. Section 147 of C.P.C. provides as under :- "147. Consent or agreement by persons under disability. - In all suits to which any person under disability is a party, any consent or agreement, as to any proceeding shall, if given or made with the express leave of the Court by the next friend or guardian for the suit, have the same force and effect as if such person, were under no disability and had given such consent or made such agreement." From the aforesaid it is apparently clear that next friend can be provided or a guardian for contesting the suit can be provided in case there is a disability. Even the provisions of Order 32 Rule 15 provides as under :- "15. Rules 1 to 14 (except Rule 2A) to apply to persons of unsound mind.- Rules Signature Not Verified SAN 1 to 14 (except Rule 2A) shall, so far as may be, apply to persons adjudged, before or during the pendency of the suit, to be of unsound mind and shall also apply to persons Digitally signed by ANINDYA SUNDAR MUKHOPADHYAY Date: 2022.04.04 14:38:37 IST who, though not so adjudged, are found by the Court on enquiry to be incapable, by 4 reason of any mental infirmity, of protecting their interest when suing or being sued." The aforesaid aspect was taken into consideration by the Hon'Â™ble Supreme Court in the case of Kasturi Bai (supra) wherein the Hon'ble Supreme has held as under :- "11. On a bare perusal of the said provision, it is evident that the Court is empowered to appoint a guardian in the event a person is adjudged to be of unsound mind. It further provided that even if a person is not so adjudged but is found by court or inquiry to be incapable to protecting his or her interest when suing or being sued or reason of any mental infirmity, an appropriate order thereunder can be passed. The respondent did not contend that appellant No.1 herein is of unsound mind. As noticed hereinbefore, the respondent herself had filed an application before the trial court for holding an inquiry to the effect that she suffers from mental infirmity." Similar view was taken by this Court in the case of Kashi Bai (supra) and has held as under :- "10..... The provisions contained in Order 32 not only empower the Court to take appropriate steps where a party to the proceedings is a minor or a person who is incapable of protecting his interest whether by reason of unsoundness of mind and mental infirmity, but also cast a mandatory duty on the Court to take steps to ensure proper representation for such persons so as to ensure that their interest in relation to the proceedings are fully protected. These provisions are a legislative recognition of the well- known principle that he State, as indeed the Court, which is part of the judicial wing of the State is in locus parentis to its citizens, who are either minors or are incapable of protecting their interests in judicial proceedings by reasons of unsoundness of mind or mental infirmity. There can, therefore, be no doubt that before the Court proceeds with a suit or other proceedings, in which one of the parties is either a minor or otherwise incapable of protecting his interests, the Court is bound to hold a preliminary enquiry and, is satisfied that the conditions of the relevant rules are attracted, to make appropriate directions with regard to the proper representation of such persons..." From the perusal of the relevant provisions that is Order 32 Rule 5, Order 32 Rule 15 and judgments passed by the Supreme Court as well as by the High Court in the aforesaid cases, it is apparently clear that the next friend can duly be appointed in case of a minor or in case of person incapable of deposing before the Court or contesting the suit. In case of a major person, the enquiry should have Signature Not Verified SAN been conducted by the learned Trial Court regarding the incapability of a person Digitally signed by ANINDYA SUNDAR MUKHOPADHYAY Date: 2022.04.04 14:38:37 IST who is willing for a next friend or a guardian. It was held in the aforesaid case that 5 the Trial Court is duty bound to hold a preliminary enquiry and if it is satisfied that the condition of the relevant rules are attracted then he is required to issue appropriate directions with regard to proper representation of such persons. In such circumstances, by the impugned order, it is reflected that the learned Trial Court has not conducted any enquiry into the matter and outrightly only on the basis of the fact that provisions of Order 32 Rule 5 does not provide for appointment of a next friend or a guardian in case of a major has rejected the application. The learned Trial Court has not even considered the provisions of Section 147 of C.P.C. prior to passing the impugned order. In such circumstances, the order impugned is unsustainable and it is hereby set aside. The matter is relegated back to the learned Trial Court for reconsideration of the application filed by the plaintiff for appointment of a next friend or guardian. He is directed to enquire into the matter with respect to the incapability of the petitioner on the ground of age as it is stated that he is mentally unfit and not in a position to even move. The application may be decided expeditiously after enquiring into the matter. Petition stands allowed. No orders as to costs. The interim order stands vacated. The Trial Court may proceed with the matter. (VISHAL MISHRA) JUDGE AM Signature Not Verified SAN Digitally signed by ANINDYA SUNDAR MUKHOPADHYAY Date: 2022.04.04 14:38:37 IST
